evidence.role: Former employee - Beer and Wine Steward
evidence.text: Good money for work expected, good employee benefits, friendly coworkers Friendly environment
Managers and leads were always super friendly and accommodating. Friendly work environment
Supportive work environment Competitive pay and benefits Opportunities for growth Team-oriented culture Flexible scheduling Great workplace with supportive team
Friendly coworkers and supportive management. Flexible scheduling for students. Easy systems to learn. Clean and organized workplace. Opportunities to move up. Good first job with supportive team
Great community, good work environment Curbie

evidence.role: Current employee - In Store Shopper - Curbside
evidence.text: Bi-yearly raises that reflect economy growth and productivity in the company. Good Short or Long Term Employment
-Benefits -Support structures for growth (Classes, apprenticeships, etc) -Community is tight nit if you can get in they'll take care of you. More than a little Disorganized.
Excellent benefits. Strong community presence. Poor leadership
Amazing growth opportunities, great leadership, good atmosphere, excellent benefits. I find it genuinely fun to work here. If you want to grow with a company, choose HEB.
good culture, career growth, i really enjoyed working with my tran good culture
Working with good partners and pay is decent if you stay to working 40-50 hours a week. Anymore, itâs not work or. Toxic upper management
Safe, secure, good pay, nice environment .
You work at a grocery store and the pay is good Racism, cut hours, HR department for Heb and more
Really good and stable job Good

I really enjoyed my time working at HEB in the deli department and it was a fun job. My co workers for the most part were cool, and it was an inclusive atmosphere. There were fun activities we did prior to the store grand opening. Received gift cards around the holidays. Discounts on HEB brand goods. My Experience Working at HEB
Overworked, Underpaid, Every Department wants you to do their responsibilities on top of yours. Overworked, Underpaid
long hours, weekends, low pay, stressful Good company
-hitting numbers can be difficult and low pay Great place to work but low pay
low pay, over worked, odd hours and toxic culture Low Pay
- Long hours - poor management - Very low pay Lots of Work for Pennies
I did not experience any cons Great company
Career growth seemed limited although there have been a few that have done really well in a short amount of time. Great culture, growth seemed limited
Daily work tasks were unclear Not much direction Solid Internship
No room for growth or opportunity to transfer to other departments to learn new skills Overall good company
Bad management Changing schedule every time Not career opportunities to growth. It was always given to their preference. Good company/ bad leadership
-little people -small -parking -location -doesnt carry everything like big stores A good part time jobs for college or a future career

Lack of hours, there are now full-time positions available for shoppers, but not for curbie positions Favortism in the department Lack of work life balance, you are expected to work within the assigned schedule, but there is always a chance that you are going to be working with a skeleton crew during closing hours Experience as a Shopper
Everyone working full time is burnt out. The benefits feel like theyâre there to keep you alive. Toxic management and work environment. No work life balance. H-E-Burnt Out
work life balance is not great sometimes Experience review
At times the weather may not be the best . Awesome

Tolerance for aggressive, old-school managers in IT with tenure, especially for the difficult store-facing engineering space. Over four years, everyday experiences of: - berating and belittling including personal insults to myself and others - demands for personal loyalty - threats of retaliation to myself and others - stated favoritism with engineering leads resulting in leadership team culture issues such as infor...

They will work you to death. L2 is the worst. Management doesnât care about you or your needs. They will talk behind your back, micromanage you until you get burnt out. Favoritism is so bad, Theyâre constantly trying to find ways to write you up or make you feel like a failure. A lot of the produce gets ruined in the returns room and put back to sell. They will make you work a week straight without days off duri...
Unfair Wages and how the company determines employees qualifications for particular roles. Unfair Wages
Hard to advance Manager favorites/racism / sexism Hard to move up
Favoritism Toxic work environment Broken machinery vital to perform job. I love what HEB could be
pay is not enough to make it a career path Austin, TX H-E-B Stores

underappreciated, ridiculous expectations, too much work and not enough support, management can't see most of your hard work outside, other PLAs are immature and often aren't working, if you don't click with the other PLA's you might be the scapegoat, if you aren't very social and cheery every day you won't get any promotion, you can get fired for calling in sick 4 times in 6 months even if you're really sick Terrib...
Curbside was HOT and sometimes too much to handle. kind of difficult to move up if you aren't friendly and buddy buddy with management Honestly Pretty good at the time
Poor communication and transparency Lacking modern competitive benefits Behind the Time
Can get really busy and can get a lot of carts in one section without any help. Fun atmosphere
